data.description:
Rec 7b of session-logs/2026-06-09-architecture-review.md (risk R6). applier.py:_write_event_file (~line 671) writes event files into the live .tickets-tracker/ worktree directly — no .ticket-write.lock, no write_commit_event — a third write mechanism beside bash write_commit_event and ticket_txn.py. The advisory pass-lock + update-ref CAS makes the COMMIT safe, but the event-file write is not serialized against a concurrent local agent's staging.

Work: extract one shared Python event-append module (filename contract I2, lock discipline I5) used by ticket_txn.py and the reconciler applier (and future strangler-fig Tier B ports). Add an import-linter-style test asserting the reconciler's only inward deps are the reducer + this module.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] One shared append implementation; applier._write_event_file routed through it (or deleted)
- [ ] Lock discipline (I5) honored for reconciler event writes; concurrency tests cover applier-vs-local-agent interleaving
- [ ] Dependency-direction test in place
data.title: Shared Python event-append module; route applier._write_event_file through it
